experiences.intentions: The active piece is an O tetromino already shown at the top center, and rotating it has no effect. The board is completely empty otherwise, so there are no immediate threats. The goal now is efficient placement to preserve a flat stack and keep the center available; since only one action is allowed this turn, the strongest progress is to drop the piece quickly. Candidate actions: left/right would only shift before later dropping, rotate does nothing for O, no_op wastes a turn, soft_drop advances only one row. Hard dropping safely places the O immediately on the empty board, which is best.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top center, with a 2x2 O stack fixed at the bottom center. The board is otherwise empty, so there is no immediate threat; the opportunity is to place the J efficiently without creating awkward overhangs. Goal is to build a flat, compact surface and preserve flexibility for upcoming S, I, Z, T pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would place the current horizontal J near center, which is acceptable but not as flat as a vertical placement beside the O. Moving left/right alone just repositions. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the J vertical with a foot to the right, which can later be shifted/dropped into a cleaner stack next to the O. So best immediate step is to rotate first.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with a settled O at bottom center and an active J high near the top in vertical orientation. Goal is to place the J to build a flat, compact stack without creating holes, ideally fitting against the existing O block. Since the piece is still very high, immediate horizontal movement is best before dropping; moving left starts guiding it toward the left side of the O where a J can later tuck in cleanly, while dropping now would commit too early and rotation is not yet necessary.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is almost empty except for a 2x2 O block centered at the bottom. The active J is high above in vertical orientation with its two-block cap to the right. Goal is to place pieces efficiently to keep the stack flat and set up future S/I/Z/T placements. Dropping the current J straight down in this orientation will land it neatly on top-left of the O, creating a low, compact structure without holes, whereas moving sideways or rotating now is less immediately useful. Hard drop best advances that sub-goal in one move.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an S near the top center. The stack below is narrow: J occupies column 4-5 around rows 15-17 and O occupies columns 4-5 at the bottom, leaving a mostly flat empty field elsewhere. My goal is to place the S to build a clean surface and avoid creating awkward overhangs above the central stack. Candidate actions: left/right shift the S horizontally before committing; rotate_right makes it vertical, which is less useful on an open board and can create a notch; soft/hard drop would commit immediately in the current position above the center. Moving left can set up the S to rest cleanly on the left side of the central stack on a future drop, preserving flexibility for the upcoming I piece. So the best immediate action is to move left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I in vertical orientation near the center. The stack is low, with a small uneven structure around columns 3-5 and plenty of open space. Best medium-term goal is to preserve flexibility and ideally set up future line clears with upcoming Z,T,L,S pieces. A vertical I is most valuable dropped into a deep column, but directly hard-dropping now in the current column would likely land on the low stack without creating a strong setup. Rotating horizontal creates options, but a single action should improve placement first. Moving left shifts the I toward the open left side where a later horizontal drop can sit flatter across the surface or a vertical drop can fill a cleaner lane. Other options: right moves toward less useful central/right space; rotate changes orientation without improving position yet; soft/hard drop commits too early. So left is the best preparatory move.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is vertical in column 4 near the top. The stack below has a small central structure with a 2-wide O base and J/S blocks, leaving broad open space on both sides. My goal now is to place the I efficiently to keep the board flat and avoid creating awkward overhangs before the upcoming Z,T,L,S sequence. Candidate actions: hard_drop would immediately place the vertical I in the center, likely creating a tall narrow spike over the existing central stack; soft_drop has the same direction but slower. Rotating to horizontal could be good later, but from this state a lateral move first is better because the current vertical lane is above the center. Moving left or right starts positioning the I toward an open side for a safer drop. Left is slightly better because the left surface is lower and wider, giving more flexibility for a future horizontal placement or vertical well use.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Since the piece is still high and there is no immediate threat, the best sub-goal is to orient it toward the likely central-right notch; rotating right turns the Z vertical, which is more flexible for threading into uneven terrain on subsequent moves. Dropping now in horizontal orientation would just land flat high above the stack and waste placement potential.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near the center. Surface below has a left-side Z stack and a long I across row 12 creating a fairly flat mid section, with lower bumps around the S/J/O on the right. Goal is to place the T toward a future fit without committing badly from spawn. Immediate candidates: hard_drop is too committal from a high spawn without horizontal adjustment; soft_drop just advances but misses a needed setup; rotations don't obviously improve more than shifting. Moving right heads the T toward the center-right contour above S/J/O where T can later rotate/drop into a useful notch, while keeping options for next moves.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top with a mostly flat open field; the stack below has a central-right notch around columns 4-6 that a T can help cover later. Immediate goal is to move the T toward the center/right while preserving flexibility, since dropping now on the far right would be inefficient and there is no urgent threat near the top. Candidate actions: hard_drop commits too early; soft_drop reduces horizontal options; rotations are not yet needed because spawn orientation is workable; left moves away from the useful center structure. experiences.intentions: The active T is high and unobstructed, with the stack concentrated low on the left/center and a relatively flat area around columns 5-7 above the S/J/O pieces. Rotations are possible, but the horizontal T is already a good fit for smoothing the topography.

Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early in a largely empty field; soft_drop also commits downward before horizontal alignment; rotations are not yet necessary because current flat-bottom L can still be repositioned. experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top center in horizontal orientation, with a fairly uneven lower stack: a 4-wide I on row 13, left Z/L clutter, central J column, and O/T on the right. Immediate threat is low since the stack is far from the top, so the priority is placement quality rather than survival. The best sub-goal is to move the S toward a useful slot; directly hard/soft dropping now would strand it high-center over mostly flat empty space and lose flexibility. Rotating vertical here does not yet exploit a cavity and may reduce options. Shifting left aims the S toward the left-center contours above the I/Z area, where upcoming L/Z/T can continue building more cleanly than locking in the middle. Candidate comparison: no_op wastes time, right moves toward the bumpier right J/O/T area without a clear fit, rotate actions don’t create an immediate better landing, drops commit too early. Therefore move left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top with empty space below; the stack is concentrated in the lower half with a relatively flat left-center surface around rows 10-13 and some bumps/right well near columns 6-9. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally fitting it onto the left-center terrain. Immediate sub-goal is to start moving the piece toward a useful landing zone before it falls further. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early from spawn and likely waste placement; soft_drop gives less lateral flexibility; rotations are not yet necessary because current orientation can still be adjusted later. Moving left shifts the L toward the broader open/left-center area and away from the more awkward right-side bumps, preserving options for later rotate/drop.

Best immediate sub-goal is to translate left toward the useful landing zone.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top with plenty of empty space below; current stack is low and uneven mainly on the left/center, with the right side relatively open. Goal is to place this Z cleanly without creating overhangs and ideally move it toward the open right side for flexible setup with upcoming T,J,O,I. Candidate actions: soft/hard drop would commit immediately in the current upper-left area, which is premature; rotations are not needed yet since horizontal Z is already easy to place and vertical Z would be narrower but not clearly better now. Moving right improves access to the open right half and preserves options for later drops.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top (rows 1-2) with empty space below, so there is no immediate collision threat. The stack is low and uneven, with some future opportunities around the center-right, but since only one action is allowed now the best sub-goal is to accelerate placement while preserving flexibility. Rotating now is unnecessary because both shown orientations are still high in open space; lateral movement is premature without seeing final target. A hard drop would commit too early, while a soft drop advances the piece safely and keeps options open for next moves.

experiences.intentions: Key state: active Z is high near the upper-right, currently in vertical orientation with cells around columns 6-7 and plenty of empty space beneath. Surface is much lower overall, with notable structures on the left/middle and a T on the bottom-right. Goal: place the Z to improve the stack without creating awkward overhangs, while preserving options for upcoming T,J,O,I. The immediate sub-goal is to move the piece toward a cleaner landing zone before dropping; rotating to horizontal now would still leave it high and not yet aligned to a target. Candidate actions: soft/hard drop would commit too early in the current column; rotate is unnecessary because current orientation can slide and later rotate if needed; right is limited at the wall and not useful. Moving left increases placement options over the central field and is the best preparatory action.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the upper-middle left, currently in vertical orientation. The stack has a noticeable cavity and uneven surface around columns 2-5, with a left-side build and a flatter landing zone lower right. Immediate goal is to place the Z to reduce overhangs and avoid creating extra holes. Rotating right to the horizontal Z orientation lets it fit more cleanly across the notch around row 7-8 than the current vertical stance. Other actions like left/right without rotating don’t improve fit yet, and dropping now in vertical orientation would leave a worse surface. So the best immediate move is to rotate clockwise.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near the top with no immediate collision pressure. The stack is uneven but low enough that survival is fine; the main opportunity is to place the T cleanly toward the center-right without creating extra overhangs. A horizontal T currently can still be shifted/rotated before committing. Hard dropping now would lock it high and waste flexibility, while soft_drop/no_op also fail to improve placement. Rotating doesn’t obviously create a better immediate fit than simply moving toward the open right side, so the best sub-goal is lateral positioning first. Moving right advances toward cleaner central-right placement over existing contours.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near the top at columns 5-7. The stack below is uneven but low enough that there is no immediate danger. The best near-term goal is to place T efficiently while preserving the central well/openings for upcoming J,O,I,Z. Direct hard drop from current spawn would likely land awkwardly on the right-side surface; first shifting left should better target the central contours around the Z/S area and keep the far right cleaner. Rotating is not yet necessary because the flat T orientation is flexible after repositioning. Among candidates, left improves placement options most; right moves away from useful cavities, soft/hard drop commits too early, and no_op wastes a move.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top around columns 4-6. The stack is uneven but the main actionable opportunity is to steer this T toward a useful placement rather than dropping immediately into the central hump. The lower board has a right-side cavity above the O/T structure and some central roughness; preserving flexibility is better than committing now. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would place too early with little alignment, no_op wastes time, rotate_right could prepare a vertical T but current spawn orientation already has options. Moving right starts aiming the piece toward the more open right-center where upcoming J,O,I,Z can also fit cleanly.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near center-right with open air below. The stack is uneven with a notable cavity around the center-left and taller right-side structures near the bottom. Goal is to place the T to improve surface flatness and avoid creating overhangs while keeping future J,O,I,Z placements flexible. Immediate drop now from current orientation would likely land awkwardly on the right hump; shifting left first moves the T toward the broader central gap and preserves rotation options next turn. Other actions: right pushes toward the wall and worse fit, rotations are premature before horizontal alignment, soft/hard drop commit too early, no_op wastes tempo.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near center with plenty of lateral freedom. Surface below has a central/right cavity around columns 4-6 above the lower stack; current spawn orientation is flat T. Goal is to place the T efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally preparing for upcoming J/O/I. Immediate sub-goal is to align the T over the central notch; moving right improves access to the cleaner center-right channel, while hard/soft drop now would commit too early and rotations are not yet clearly superior. Left moves toward the taller left stack, so right is best.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high with ample lateral space, and the stack below has a notable valley around columns 5-6 above the lower right structures plus some unevenness on the left. Immediate goal is to place the T to improve surface quality without creating overhangs. A simple downward move now is strongest because hard_drop would commit from a suboptimal spawn column, while left/right or rotation can be decided after seeing the descent path; there is no urgent threat near the top. soft_drop advances toward a future tuck into the center-right gap and preserves flexibility better than no_op.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high at the top with empty space beneath; no immediate threat from stack height. The board surface around the stack has useful structure in the center-left/right, but dropping now from current column 4 would likely create an awkward overhang unless repositioned first. Our goal is to place the J to support future line clears while avoiding isolated holes. Rotating right to vertical-with-foot orientation (rotation 1) is a good setup because it can later be shifted into a cleaner tuck than the flat spawn shape; compared with left/right or dropping now, rotation preserves time and improves fit options before descent.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the center-right in a vertical orientation. Surface below has a useful notch around columns 4-6 above the mid-stack; immediate hard drop from current orientation would likely land awkwardly on the uneven top and reduce future flexibility. Goal is to place the J to support a flatter surface and potentially complement upcoming O/I. Candidate moves: left shifts toward the central depression and gives more placement options; right moves toward the wall with less utility; rotations could help later but first aligning horizontally over the center is strongest; dropping now is premature. Best immediate sub-goal is reposition left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the top in vertical orientation with the board mostly open there; no immediate threat at the spawn area. The stack below has uneven columns, with a notable left-side structure and central cavities, so the current priority is to start moving this J toward a useful placement rather than dropping immediately in a poor central spot. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early before alignment; rotations don't create a clearly better immediate fit yet; right moves away from the more accessible left structures. Moving left begins steering the J toward the left-side contour where it can later tuck and support flatter stacking.

experiences.intentions: Active J is in rotation 1, near the upper-left/mid-left with empty space below until existing stack around columns 1-4. The surface below has a left-side cavity and a taller center-right stack; immediate threat is low because board top is very open. Goal is to place J efficiently to support future O/I/Z/J without creating holes. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit current vertical J at x≈3 onto the uneven left-center surface, likely leaving awkward shape; soft_drop similarly just advances without improving alignment. Rotating could make a horizontal J that may fit shelves, but from current position there is room first to shift. Moving left heads toward the left wall/column 1-2 area where a J can tuck and help fill the open trench above the L stack, which seems more valuable than moving right into open center. So best immediate sub-goal is translate left before locking.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the upper left in rotation 1, with open space directly below and a jagged stack concentrated lower on the board. No immediate threat near the top, so the goal is efficient placement to build flat surfaces and avoid creating overhangs. A hard drop now would place the J in its current column too far left with limited shaping value. Moving right can aim the J toward the central wells above the stack, giving more flexible future placements with upcoming O/I/Z/J pieces. Rotating is less urgent because current orientation already fits through open air, and soft drop/no_op waste tempo. Best immediate sub-goal is reposition horizontally before committing downward.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the upper-left/mid area. Surface below has a left-side cavity around columns 2-4, but immediate hard drop from current vertical orientation likely lands awkwardly on the existing J/Z/T stack. Valid rotations show rotation 0 creates a horizontal JJJ with tail on left, which is more suitable for sliding into the broad flat area near the top of the current stack. Goal is to place the piece safely and start flattening the uneven skyline without creating overhangs. Among single moves, rotate_left changes from current rotation 1 to rotation 0, improving future placement options before gravity/next moves; left/right now are less urgent than getting the useful orientation.

experiences.intentions: Active O piece is at the very top centered around columns 4-5 with no immediate rotation benefit. The stack below has a relatively open center but some unevenness; O cannot rotate, so the meaningful choices are horizontal movement or dropping. Goal is to place this O cleanly to reduce surface roughness and avoid creating holes. Directly hard dropping from current column appears safe and efficient, likely landing on the central support around the S/J area without extra overhang. Left/right first could seek a side notch, but with only one move this turn, immediate progress toward a stable placement is best.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I at the very top, currently horizontal across columns 3-6 with empty space below. The stack is uneven but not near top-out; the clearest immediate opportunity is to place the I vertically into the open central well around column 4/5 to improve future line clears and avoid leaving a floating horizontal placement near the top. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would leave the I horizontal on upper surface and likely create overhang; left/right only shift before committing but vertical alignment is the more valuable sub-goal first; soft_drop is premature before orienting. Rotating right turns the I vertical in an open area with no collision, advancing toward a clean drop into the gap next turn.
